name: GSOC subscriptions only work on full Bee nodes and require mutable stamps

links.targetTitle: Bee Reactor Adapter requires full Bee node, mutable stamp, wallet, and open P2P ports
Feed-backing stamps should be mutable to avoid bucket exhaustion from frequent updates

global.title: GSOC subscriptions only work on full Bee nodes and require mutable stamps
global.content:
## Claim

GSOC notifications have two hard constraints: the receiving node must be a **full node** (light nodes do not sync neighborhood chunks) and writers must use **mutable stamps** (to avoid bucket exhaustion on frequent signals).

## Evidence

- Light nodes do not store neighborhood chunks — they cannot see incoming SOC updates addressed to their neighborhood, so GSOC subscription is a full-node-only feature
- Each GSOC send is a new SOC = a new chunk consuming a bucket slot. With immutable stamps, frequent notifications hot-spot a bucket and fail the batch well before TTL
- Mutable stamps replace the oldest chunk in the overflowing bucket, so the notification channel self-garbage-collects

## Why it matters

Any architecture that uses GSOC for collaboration coordination (e.g. "Connect instances register with a switchboard via GSOC") has two environmental preconditions that affect deployment: the receiving instance must run a full node, and the stamp provisioning flow must request `immutableFlag: false`. Both are easy to get wrong silently.
global.description: Light nodes do not sync neighborhood chunks, so they cannot receive GSOC updates. Writers must use mutable stamps to avoid bucket exhaustion from frequent signals.
